{"problem_standardized_math":"Using the compact limited-memory BFGS representation\n\\[\nB=\\xi I+WMW^T, \\tag{19.29}\n\\]\ndecompose the primal-dual matrix as\n\\[\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\xi I & 0 & A_E^T & A_I^T \\\\\n0 & \\Sigma & 0 & I \\\\\nA_E & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\nA_I & I & 0 & 0\n\\end{bmatrix}\n+\n\\begin{bmatrix}\nW \\\\\n0 \\\\\n0 \\\\\n0\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\begin{bmatrix}\nMW^T & 0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{bmatrix}. \\tag{19.60}\n\\]\nUse the Sherman--Morrison--Woodbury formula to express the inverse of the matrix in \\((19.60)\\). Then show that computing the primal-dual step\n\\[\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\nabla_{xx}^2 \\mathcal L & 0 & A_E^T(x) & A_I^T(x) \\\\\n0 & \\Sigma & 0 & -I \\\\\nA_E(x) & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\nA_I(x) & -I & 0 & 0\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\begin{bmatrix}\np_x \\\\\np_s \\\\\n-p_y \\\\\n-p_z\n\\end{bmatrix}\n=\n-\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\nabla f(x)-A_E^T(x)y-A_I^T(x)z \\\\\nz-\\mu S^{-1}e \\\\\nc_E(x) \\\\\nc_I(x)-s\n\\end{bmatrix} \\tag{19.12}\n\\]\nrequires solving linear systems of the form \\(Cv=b\\), where \\(C\\) is the left matrix in \\((19.60)\\) and \\(v\\) and \\(b\\) are appropriate vectors."}
